title: Google introduces Gemma 4 12B for local multimodal AI on laptops
friendlyTitle: Gemma 4 12B brings multimodal AI closer to local laptops
summary: Google DeepMind introduced Gemma 4 12B, an Apache 2.0 multimodal model designed to run locally with around 16GB of VRAM or unified memory. The model uses a unified, encoder-free architecture for text, vision, and native audio inputs, targeting local agentic workflows and offline use cases.
friendlySummary: Google’s Gemma 4 12B is an open model that can work with text, images, and audio and is designed to run on everyday developer hardware.
whyItMatters: Local multimodal models are useful when privacy, cost, latency, or offline access matter. A capable mid-sized model that can handle text, images, and audio on consumer hardware gives developers more room to build personal and business tools without sending every task to the cloud.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Local AI can be cheaper, faster, and more private. This gives builders another option for tools that should work on-device or offline.
